10.3 Survivors.d: Social protection spending for survivors (widows, widowers, and orphans), including bereavement benefits, widowed parent’s allowance, bereavement support payment, and war widows’ pensions.
10.3 Survivors.b: Widows, widowers, orphans, bereaved families, and surviving dependants of deceased workers or service personnel

Single Financial Guidance Body.d: Now operating as the Money and Pensions Service (MaPS), this body provides free financial guidance to the public. It merged the Money Advice Service, The Pensions Advisory Service, and Pension Wise into a single organisation.
Single Financial Guidance Body.b: Consumers seeking financial advice, pension holders, people in debt, and those needing money guidance

Net Zero North Sea Storage Limited.d: A government-backed company established to support carbon capture and storage (CCS) infrastructure in the North Sea. It facilitates CO2 transport and storage networks as part of the UK’s net zero carbon strategy.
Net Zero North Sea Storage Limited.b: The energy transition sector, CCS technology developers, coastal communities, and future generations through climate mitigation

Heritage Lottery Fund.d: Now part of The National Lottery Heritage Fund, it distributes National Lottery money to heritage projects across the UK. Funds historic buildings restoration, natural heritage, cultural traditions, community heritage, and museums.
Heritage Lottery Fund.b: Heritage organisations, historic buildings, museums, community groups, and the public through preserved heritage

Community Health Partnership.d: A Scottish Government body (CHP) that managed primary care facilities and community health infrastructure in Scotland. It delivered and maintained local health centres and GP premises through public-private partnerships.
Community Health Partnership.b: Scottish NHS patients, GP practices, community health services, and local healthcare workers

1.6 General public services n.e.c..d: General public services spending not elsewhere classified, including government contingency funds, machinery of government changes, unallocated reserves, and public service obligations that do not fit other COFOG categories.
1.6 General public services n.e.c..b: Government departments and indirectly all citizens through general public service provision

Independent Parliamentary Standards Auth.d: IPSA was created after the 2009 MP expenses scandal to independently regulate and administer MPs’ pay, pensions, and business costs. It replaced the discredited self-regulation system for parliamentary expenses.
Independent Parliamentary Standards Auth.b: Members of Parliament (through legitimate expenses), and the public through transparent, accountable expenses regulation

10.7 Social exclusion n.e.c.; of which:.d: Personal social services spending to address social exclusion, including outreach programmes, social inclusion projects, community-based support services, and targeted interventions for marginalised groups delivered by local authorities.
10.7 Social exclusion n.e.c.; of which:.b: Socially excluded individuals, homeless people, those with substance abuse issues, and marginalised communities

5.4 Protection of biodiversity and lands.d: Government spending on conserving biodiversity, protecting natural habitats, maintaining national parks, Areas of Outstanding Natural Beauty, Sites of Special Scientific Interest, and landscape preservation programmes.
5.4 Protection of biodiversity and lands.b: Wildlife and ecosystems, visitors to protected areas, conservation organisations, and communities near national parks

National Savings and Investments.d: NS&I is the government’s savings bank, offering savings and investment products backed by HM Treasury. Products include Premium Bonds, Income Bonds, and Direct ISAs. It provides a cost-effective source of government financing.
National Savings and Investments.b: Individual savers, Premium Bond holders, and the government through cost-effective public debt financing

9.5 Education not definable by level.d: Education spending that cannot be attributed to a specific level (primary, secondary, tertiary), including cross-cutting education programmes, special educational needs provision, pupil referral units, and multi-level educational initiatives.
9.5 Education not definable by level.b: Students with special educational needs, pupils in alternative provision, and learners in cross-level programmes

Coal Authority.d: The Coal Authority manages the legacy of coal mining in the UK, including subsidence claims, mine water treatment, tip safety, mine entry records, and contaminated land remediation at former colliery sites. Created by the Coal Industry Act 1994.
Coal Authority.b: Former mining communities, property owners affected by subsidence, and the environment through mine water treatment

United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority P.d: Pension schemes for current and former employees of UKAEA, covering the Combined Pension Scheme and related arrangements for staff who worked in UK nuclear research and development.
United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority P.b: Retired UKAEA employees, current UKAEA pension scheme members, and their dependants

4.2 of which: market support under CAP.d: UK expenditure on Common Agricultural Policy market support mechanisms (pre-Brexit) and successor domestic agricultural payment schemes, including direct payments to farmers, price intervention, and market stabilisation measures.
4.2 of which: market support under CAP.b: Farmers, agricultural producers, rural landowners, and the farming sector broadly

Northern Ireland Transport Holding Compa.d: NITHC is the parent company of Translink, Northern Ireland’s public transport operator. It oversees Metro (Belfast buses), Ulsterbus (regional buses), and NI Railways, providing integrated public transport across Northern Ireland.
Northern Ireland Transport Holding Compa.b: Public transport users in Northern Ireland, commuters, rural communities, and transport workers

5.5 R&D environment protection.d: Research and development spending on environmental protection, including climate science, environmental monitoring technology, pollution research, and ecosystem studies funded through NERC and other research bodies.
5.5 R&D environment protection.b: Environmental researchers, climate scientists, conservation practitioners, and future generations through environmental knowledge

Pool Reinsurance Company Limited.d: Pool Re is a government-backed terrorism reinsurance scheme established after the IRA’s Bishopsgate bombing. It provides reinsurance to insurers for commercial property damage from terrorist attacks, ensuring terrorism insurance remains available.
Pool Reinsurance Company Limited.b: Commercial property owners, insurance companies, businesses, and the economy through terrorism risk coverage

6.2 Community development.d: Spending on community development programmes including neighbourhood renewal, community empowerment, regeneration initiatives, community centres, and local development projects. Covers both urban and rural community investment.
6.2 Community development.b: Local communities, community organisations, residents of deprived areas, and community development workers

8.1 Recreational and sporting services.d: Government spending on recreational facilities and sporting services including parks, playgrounds, sports facilities, swimming pools, and outdoor recreation. Covers both capital investment and running costs at local and national levels.
8.1 Recreational and sporting services.b: Sports participants, park users, recreational facility visitors, athletes, and local communities

House of Lords.d: Spending on the House of Lords including peers’ allowances, the Lords Chamber administration, committees, library and research services, facilities management, and the constitutional role of the upper chamber of Parliament.
House of Lords.b: Members of the House of Lords, parliamentary staff, and the legislative process

Territorial, Auxiliary and Volunteer Res.d: TRAVRAs (now Reserve Forces’ and Cadets’ Associations) support the Reserve Forces and Cadets across the UK. They manage training estates, cadet organisations, and provide administrative and logistical support to reserve military units.
Territorial, Auxiliary and Volunteer Res.b: Military reservists, cadets, armed forces veterans, and young people in cadet organisations

The Pensions Regulator.d: TPR regulates workplace pensions in the UK, ensuring employers comply with auto-enrolment duties and that defined benefit pension schemes are properly funded. It can impose penalties and has anti-avoidance powers.
The Pensions Regulator.b: Workplace pension scheme members, employees auto-enrolled in pensions, and pension scheme trustees

Children and Family Court Advisory and S.d: Cafcass represents children in family court cases in England. It provides independent social work advice to courts on children’s welfare in cases involving divorce, separation, care proceedings, and adoption.
Children and Family Court Advisory and S.b: Children involved in family court proceedings, families undergoing separation, and family court judges

Royal Household.d: Government funding for the official duties of the Sovereign and Royal Household, now primarily through the Sovereign Grant. Covers official travel, property maintenance (Royal Palaces), staffing, and ceremonial duties.
Royal Household.b: The Royal Family in their official capacity, visitors to Royal Palaces, and the tourism industry

4.2 of which: forestry.d: Government spending on forestry including the Forestry Commission (England), Forestry and Land Scotland, Natural Resources Wales forestry operations, tree planting programmes, woodland management, and timber production.
4.2 of which: forestry.b: Forest visitors, timber industry, rural communities, biodiversity, and the environment through carbon sequestration

English Heritage.d: Now split into Historic England (government body) and English Heritage Trust (charity, 2015). Manages over 400 historic properties, monuments, and sites in England. Provides heritage protection, conservation advice, and public access to historic places.
English Heritage.b: Visitors to historic sites, heritage professionals, property owners in conservation areas, and the tourism industry

United Kingdom Sports Council.d: Known as UK Sport, it invests in Olympic and Paralympic sports to achieve medal success at major games. It distributes National Lottery and Exchequer funding to elite athletes and national governing bodies of sport.
United Kingdom Sports Council.b: Elite athletes, Olympic and Paralympic competitors, national sports governing bodies, and high-performance coaches

Diamond Light Source Limited.d: The UK’s national synchrotron science facility located at Harwell, Oxfordshire. It produces intense beams of light used for scientific research in physics, chemistry, biology, engineering, and materials science across academia and industry.
Diamond Light Source Limited.b: Scientists, researchers, pharmaceutical companies, materials engineers, and academic institutions

National Museum of Science and Industry.d: Now operating as the Science Museum Group, it comprises the Science Museum (London), National Railway Museum (York), Science and Industry Museum (Manchester), National Science and Media Museum (Bradford), and Locomotion (Shildon).
National Museum of Science and Industry.b: Museum visitors, school groups, science enthusiasts, researchers, and the general public

Sport England Lottery.d: The National Lottery distribution fund managed by Sport England, investing in grassroots sport and physical activity across England. Funds community sports facilities, coaching, participation programmes, and active lifestyle initiatives.
Sport England Lottery.b: Grassroots sports participants, community sports clubs, sports volunteers, and people becoming physically active

Office for Standards in Education, Child.d: Ofsted inspects and regulates services providing education and skills for learners of all ages, and services providing care for children and young people. It publishes inspection reports and sets quality standards for schools, colleges, and childcare.
Office for Standards in Education, Child.b: School pupils, parents, teachers, childcare providers, college students, and the education system

Ministry of Justice: Judicial Pensions S.d: The pension scheme for judges and judicial office holders in England and Wales, providing defined benefit pensions for members of the judiciary upon retirement from the bench.
Ministry of Justice: Judicial Pensions S.b: Retired judges, serving judicial office holders, and their dependants

Arts Council of England Lottery.d: National Lottery funds distributed by Arts Council England to support arts and cultural projects. Funds individual artists, arts organisations, museums, libraries, and creative projects across England through grants programmes.
Arts Council of England Lottery.b: Artists, arts organisations, cultural venues, creative practitioners, and communities accessing arts activities

1.2 Foreign economic aid.d: UK Official Development Assistance (ODA) spending, including bilateral aid to developing countries, contributions to multilateral organisations (World Bank, UN agencies), humanitarian relief, and development programmes managed by FCDO.
1.2 Foreign economic aid.b: Developing nations, populations in poverty, humanitarian crisis victims, and international development organisations

3.6 Public order and safety n.e.c..d: Public order and safety spending not elsewhere classified, including civil defence, emergency planning, coroners’ services, and miscellaneous public safety programmes not covered by police, fire, courts, or prisons categories.
3.6 Public order and safety n.e.c..b: All citizens through public safety, emergency planners, coroners, and communities affected by emergencies

Genomics England Limited.d: A Department of Health-owned company that ran the 100,000 Genomes Project and now manages the NHS Genomic Medicine Service research infrastructure. It sequences and analyses genomes to advance precision medicine and genomic healthcare.
Genomics England Limited.b: Patients with rare diseases and cancer, genomic researchers, the NHS, and future patients through personalised medicine

Libraries (Subgroup).d: Government spending on public library services, including local authority library provision, the British Library, and library development programmes. Covers book stocks, digital resources, premises, and library staff.
Libraries (Subgroup).b: Library users, readers, researchers, students, communities relying on libraries for digital access and information

Tate Gallery.d: The Tate Gallery network comprises Tate Britain (British art from 1500), Tate Modern (international modern art), Tate Liverpool, and Tate St Ives. It is one of the UK’s largest art institutions, holding a national collection of British and modern art.
Tate Gallery.b: Art lovers, gallery visitors, artists, art students, researchers, and international tourists

Competition and Markets Authority.d: The CMA is the UK’s primary competition and consumer protection authority. It investigates mergers, market studies, cartels, and anti-competitive practices. Post-Brexit it assumed additional powers previously held by the European Commission for UK matters.
Competition and Markets Authority.b: Consumers through fair pricing and competition, businesses competing fairly, and the economy through efficient markets

Fleetbank Funding Limited.d: A government-owned special purpose vehicle established during the financial crisis for managing legacy financial assets, related to HM Treasury’s interventions in the banking sector.
Fleetbank Funding Limited.b: Taxpayers through management and recovery of financial crisis-era public assets

East West Rail.d: A major rail infrastructure project to create a direct rail link between Oxford, Milton Keynes, Bedford, and Cambridge. It aims to improve connectivity across the Oxford-Cambridge arc and support housing and economic growth in the region.
East West Rail.b: Commuters and travellers in the Oxford-Cambridge corridor, local businesses, housing developers, and regional economies

Care Quality Commission.d: The CQC is the independent regulator of health and adult social care in England. It inspects and rates hospitals, GP practices, care homes, dental practices, and home care agencies to ensure they meet fundamental quality and safety standards.
Care Quality Commission.b: Patients, care home residents, people receiving home care, healthcare providers, and families choosing care services

Reclaim Fund Ltd.d: Now known as Dormant Assets Fund Ltd, it manages dormant bank and building society accounts. After attempting to reunite owners with their money, residual funds are released to the National Lottery Community Fund for social and environmental purposes.
Reclaim Fund Ltd.b: Account holders reunited with dormant assets, charities and social enterprises funded through released dormant assets

Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew.d: Kew Gardens is a world-leading botanical research institution and UNESCO World Heritage Site in southwest London. It maintains living collections, a seed bank (Millennium Seed Bank), herbarium, and conducts research on plant science and conservation.
Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew.b: Visitors, botanical scientists, conservationists, plant breeders, and global biodiversity through seed banking

National Audit Office.d: The NAO scrutinises public spending for Parliament, auditing government departments, agencies, and public bodies. It produces value-for-money reports and supports the Public Accounts Committee in holding government to account.
National Audit Office.b: Parliament, taxpayers, government departments (through improved efficiency), and the public through accountability

Youth Justice Board for England and Wale.d: The YJB oversees the youth justice system, commissioning the secure youth estate, setting standards for youth offending teams, and working to prevent youth crime. It manages young offender institutions, secure training centres, and secure children’s homes.
Youth Justice Board for England and Wale.b: Young offenders, at-risk youth, communities affected by youth crime, and youth justice professionals

National Citizen Service.d: NCS is a voluntary personal development programme for 15-17 year olds in England, offering outdoor activities, skills development, and community action projects. Originally a flagship Big Society programme under the Cameron government.
National Citizen Service.b: Teenagers aged 15-17, youth workers, community organisations benefiting from NCS social action projects

2.5 Defence n.e.c..d: Defence spending not elsewhere classified, including military pensions, veterans’ services, defence estates management, and miscellaneous defence expenditure not covered by specific military or civil defence categories.
2.5 Defence n.e.c..b: Military personnel, veterans, defence estate communities, and the armed forces broadly

British Transport Police Authority.d: The BTPA oversees the British Transport Police, which is responsible for policing the railways and railway stations across England, Wales, and Scotland. It ensures safety and security on the rail network including the London Underground.
British Transport Police Authority.b: Rail passengers, railway staff, commuters, and the travelling public using rail and underground services

Serious Fraud Office.d: The SFO investigates and prosecutes the most serious and complex cases of fraud, bribery, and corruption in England, Wales, and Northern Ireland. It has unique powers to compel witnesses and seize documents.
Serious Fraud Office.b: Victims of serious fraud, the financial system, businesses, and the public through deterrence of economic crime

British Film Institute.d: The BFI is the lead organisation for film in the UK, distributing National Lottery funding for film production, managing the BFI National Archive, operating BFI Southbank cinema, and running the BFI Player streaming service.
British Film Institute.b: Filmmakers, cinema audiences, film students, the UK film industry, and cultural heritage through film preservation

Enrichment Holdings Ltd.d: A government-owned company that held the UK government’s residual interest in Urenco, the uranium enrichment consortium. It manages legacy assets and liabilities from the UK’s involvement in uranium enrichment operations.
Enrichment Holdings Ltd.b: Taxpayers through management of nuclear industry legacy assets, and the nuclear energy sector

National Lottery: UK Sport Lottery.d: National Lottery funds allocated specifically through UK Sport for elite sport investment, funding World Class Performance programmes, major events hosting, and international sporting influence.
National Lottery: UK Sport Lottery.b: Elite athletes, Olympic and Paralympic sports, international event organisers, and high-performance sport programmes

Parliamentary Works Grant.d: Dedicated funding stream for the restoration and renewal of the Palace of Westminster, addressing critical maintenance backlog including fire safety, mechanical and electrical systems, and structural repairs to the Grade I listed building.
Parliamentary Works Grant.b: Parliament, the democratic process, heritage conservation, and construction workers on the restoration project

Air Travel Trust.d: The ATT holds funds from the Air Travel Organisers’ Licensing (ATOL) scheme managed by the Civil Aviation Authority. It provides financial protection to holidaymakers when travel companies collapse, funding repatriation and refunds.
Air Travel Trust.b: Package holiday travellers, air passengers, and consumers booking with ATOL-protected travel companies

Independent Police Complaints Commission.d: Now replaced by the Independent Office for Police Conduct (IOPC, 2018). It was the body responsible for overseeing complaints against police forces in England and Wales, conducting independent investigations into serious police misconduct and deaths in custody.
Independent Police Complaints Commission.b: Members of the public with complaints against police, victims of police misconduct, and police accountability

National Assembly for Wales.d: Now known as the Senedd Cymru / Welsh Parliament (renamed 2020). The devolved legislature for Wales with law-making powers in areas including health, education, environment, transport, and local government for Wales.
National Assembly for Wales.b: The people of Wales, Welsh elected members (Senedd Members), and Welsh democratic governance

Imperial War Museum.d: IWM is a family of five museums covering conflicts from World War I to the present: IWM London, IWM North (Manchester), IWM Duxford (aviation), Churchill War Rooms, and HMS Belfast. It preserves and communicates the stories of modern conflict.
Imperial War Museum.b: Museum visitors, veterans, military historians, school groups, researchers, and international tourists

The Planning Inspectorate.d: PINS processes planning and enforcement appeals, examines local plans, and handles nationally significant infrastructure project applications in England. It provides an independent appeal mechanism against local authority planning decisions.
The Planning Inspectorate.b: Planning applicants, property developers, local communities, local authorities, and infrastructure project promoters

National Institute for Health and Care E.d: NICE provides evidence-based guidance and advice on health and social care treatments, clinical practice, and public health. Its technology appraisals determine which treatments the NHS should fund, significantly influencing drug availability.
National Institute for Health and Care E.b: NHS patients, clinicians, pharmaceutical companies (through market access), and the health system through cost-effective care

Salix.d: Salix Finance Ltd provides interest-free loans and grants to public sector organisations for energy efficiency and carbon reduction projects. It funds LED lighting, boiler upgrades, insulation, and renewable installations in schools, hospitals, and government buildings.
Salix.b: Public sector organisations, schools, hospitals, universities, and the environment through reduced carbon emissions

Advisory, Conciliation and Arbitration S.d: ACAS provides free and impartial information, advice, training, and conciliation services to employers and employees on workplace relations, employment law, and dispute resolution. It offers early conciliation before employment tribunal claims.
Advisory, Conciliation and Arbitration S.b: Employers, employees, trade unions, HR professionals, and people involved in workplace disputes

8.4 Religious and other community servic.d: Government spending on religious and community services, including support for religious buildings maintenance (churches, mosques, etc.), interfaith programmes, community cohesion initiatives, and community-based civil society organisations.
8.4 Religious and other community servic.b: Religious communities, faith organisations, community groups, and residents benefiting from community cohesion programmes

National Museums Liverpool.d: A group of museums and galleries in Liverpool including the World Museum, Walker Art Gallery, Merseyside Maritime Museum, International Slavery Museum, Lady Lever Art Gallery, Sudley House, and Museum of Liverpool.
National Museums Liverpool.b: Museum visitors in Liverpool, school groups, researchers, tourists, and the Liverpool cultural economy

National Maritime Museum.d: Part of Royal Museums Greenwich, it is the UK’s leading maritime museum. The campus includes the National Maritime Museum, Royal Observatory Greenwich, the Queen’s House, and the Cutty Sark. It holds major maritime art and navigation collections.
National Maritime Museum.b: Museum visitors, maritime historians, school groups, tourists, and the Greenwich cultural area

8.5 R&D recreation, culture and religion.d: Research and development spending related to recreation, culture, and religion, including arts and humanities research, cultural heritage science, sports science, and cultural policy research funded through AHRC and other bodies.
8.5 R&D recreation, culture and religion.b: Arts and humanities researchers, cultural heritage scientists, sports scientists, and academic institutions

Institute for Apprenticeship.d: Now the Institute for Apprenticeships and Technical Education (IfATE), it oversees the quality of apprenticeship standards and technical qualifications in England. Employer-led, it approves apprenticeship standards and assessment plans.
Institute for Apprenticeship.b: Apprentices, employers offering apprenticeships, training providers, and the skills economy

Commonwealth Scholarship Commission.d: The CSC manages the UK’s contribution to the Commonwealth Scholarship and Fellowship Plan, providing scholarships for postgraduate students and academics from Commonwealth countries to study at UK universities.
Commonwealth Scholarship Commission.b: Commonwealth country students, UK universities, and international academic exchange and development

National Endowment for Science, Technolo.d: Nesta (originally NESTA) is an innovation foundation that acts as an investor and foundation for social innovation. It uses a mix of programmes, investment, policy research, and practical tools to help solve major societal challenges.
National Endowment for Science, Technolo.b: Social innovators, start-ups, charities, public services seeking innovation, and communities benefiting from innovative solutions

The National Archives.d: TNA is the official archive and publisher for the UK government. It holds over 1,000 years of government records, provides public access to historical documents, sets standards for records management across government, and manages the Freedom of Information process.
The National Archives.b: Historians, genealogists, researchers, government departments, legal professionals, and the public accessing records

Visit Britain.d: VisitBritain is the national tourism agency, promoting Britain internationally as a tourist destination. It works with the tourism industry to grow inbound visitor numbers and spending, and provides tourism market intelligence.
Visit Britain.b: The tourism industry, hotels, attractions, international visitors, and regional economies dependent on tourism

5.3 Pollution abatement.d: Government spending on reducing and controlling pollution including air quality monitoring, noise abatement, soil contamination remediation, and industrial emissions control. Covers Environment Agency regulatory activities and Clean Air Zone programmes.
5.3 Pollution abatement.b: Communities affected by pollution, the environment, public health, and industries required to meet pollution standards

British Tourist Authority.d: The BTA is the statutory body (trading as VisitBritain) responsible for promoting tourism to and within Britain. It markets British destinations internationally, conducts tourism research, and supports the tourism industry.
British Tourist Authority.b: Tourism businesses, hospitality industry, regional economies, international visitors, and domestic tourists

2.3 Foreign military aid.d: UK spending on military aid to foreign nations, including defence equipment transfers, military training programmes for partner nations, and contributions to international military operations and peacekeeping missions.
2.3 Foreign military aid.b: Partner nation armed forces, international peacekeeping operations, allied countries, and global security

8.3 Broadcasting and publishing services.d: Government spending on broadcasting and publishing, including the BBC World Service funding, government publications, S4C Welsh language broadcasting, and regulatory costs of Ofcom’s broadcasting functions.
8.3 Broadcasting and publishing services.b: BBC World Service audiences, Welsh language viewers, publishing industry, and the global audience for UK broadcasting

4.7 Other industries.d: Government spending on industries not classified elsewhere, including support for specific manufacturing sectors, industrial strategy interventions, sector deals, and government assistance to strategically important industries.
4.7 Other industries.b: Specific industrial sectors, manufacturing businesses, and workers in supported industries

Departmental unallocated provision.d: Treasury reserves and contingency funding not yet allocated to specific departmental programmes. This represents held-back spending capacity within departmental budgets awaiting allocation to emerging priorities or unforeseen costs.
Departmental unallocated provision.b: Government departments requiring additional funding, and the public through fiscal flexibility

Northern Powerhouse Investments Limited.d: A government-backed investment company focused on supporting economic growth in northern England through equity and debt investments in businesses across the Northern Powerhouse region.
Northern Powerhouse Investments Limited.b: Businesses in northern England, entrepreneurs, regional economies, and communities in the North

Oak National Academy Limited.d: An arm’s-length body providing free online curriculum resources for teachers in England. Originally created during COVID-19 as a virtual school, it was formalised as a permanent independent body providing lesson plans, videos, and teaching materials.
Oak National Academy Limited.b: Teachers, school pupils, parents supporting home learning, and schools seeking curriculum resources

Office of Gas and Electricity Markets.d: Ofgem regulates the gas and electricity markets in Great Britain. It protects consumers, ensures security of energy supply, oversees network companies, administers environmental programmes (like the Renewables Obligation), and sets price caps.
Office of Gas and Electricity Markets.b: Energy consumers, gas and electricity customers, vulnerable energy users, and the energy market

UK Asset Resolution.d: UKAR was the holding company for Bradford & Bingley and NRAM (Northern Rock Asset Management), managing the government-owned mortgage books acquired during the 2008 financial crisis. It wound down these legacy assets on behalf of taxpayers.
UK Asset Resolution.b: Taxpayers through recovery of financial crisis costs, and existing mortgage holders in the legacy books

Flood Re.d: A joint government and insurance industry scheme making flood insurance more affordable and available in high-risk areas. It acts as a reinsurer, capping flood insurance premiums for domestic properties based on council tax bands.
Flood Re.b: Homeowners in flood-risk areas, insurance companies, and communities vulnerable to flooding

Independent Commission for Reconciliatio.d: ICRIR was established under the Northern Ireland Troubles (Legacy and Reconciliation) Act 2023 to investigate Troubles-related deaths and serious injuries. It replaced previous legacy investigation mechanisms with a single independent body.
Independent Commission for Reconciliatio.b: Victims and families of the Northern Ireland Troubles, and Northern Irish society through truth recovery and reconciliation

Office of the Parliamentary Commissioner.d: The Parliamentary and Health Service Ombudsman (PHSO) investigates complaints about UK government departments, agencies, and the NHS in England. It is the final stage for complaints that have not been resolved by the organisation concerned.
Office of the Parliamentary Commissioner.b: Citizens with unresolved complaints against government or NHS, and public service improvement through complaint findings

UK Film Council Lottery.d: National Lottery funds previously distributed by the UK Film Council (abolished 2011, functions transferred to BFI) for British film production, distribution, education, and audience development.
UK Film Council Lottery.b: British filmmakers, independent cinema, film audiences, and the UK film industry

Aggregator Vehicle PLC.d: A government-owned company that aggregates smaller government lending schemes and programmes for more efficient capital markets funding. It issues bonds to finance public sector lending activities.
Aggregator Vehicle PLC.b: Government lending programmes, public sector borrowers, and taxpayers through more efficient debt management

Northern Ireland Trust Ports.d: Trust ports in Northern Ireland (Belfast, Londonderry, Warrenpoint, etc.) that are independent statutory bodies operating commercial port facilities. They are not owned by shareholders or local authorities but managed in trust for stakeholders.
Northern Ireland Trust Ports.b: Shipping companies, importers/exporters, Northern Irish trade, port workers, and the regional economy

Channel Four Television Corporation.d: Channel 4 is a publicly owned, commercially funded UK public service broadcaster. It commissions content from independent producers rather than making programmes in-house, stimulating the UK independent production sector.
Channel Four Television Corporation.b: Television viewers, independent production companies, diverse content creators, and advertisers

Knowledge Transfer Network Limited.d: KTN (now part of Innovate UK Business Connect) connects businesses with research organisations, funding, and each other to accelerate innovation. It operates across sectors, facilitating knowledge exchange and collaborative R&D.
Knowledge Transfer Network Limited.b: Innovative businesses, researchers, universities, and SMEs seeking technology partnerships

Royal Hospital, Chelsea.d: The Royal Hospital Chelsea is a retirement home for former soldiers of the British Army (Chelsea Pensioners). Founded by Charles II, it houses around 300 veterans who wear distinctive scarlet uniforms. Funded by the MoD.
Royal Hospital, Chelsea.b: Chelsea Pensioners (retired soldiers), military veterans, and the heritage of British military care

The Gambling Commission.d: The Gambling Commission regulates commercial gambling in Great Britain including casinos, betting, bingo, lotteries, and online gambling. It issues licences, sets standards, and protects consumers from gambling-related harm.
The Gambling Commission.b: Gambling consumers, problem gamblers (through protection), the gambling industry, and society through regulated gambling

Parole Board for England and Wales.d: The Parole Board is an independent body that assesses whether prisoners can be safely released into the community. It conducts risk assessments and oral hearings to determine if continued detention is necessary for public protection.
Parole Board for England and Wales.b: Prisoners eligible for parole, victims (through public protection), and the criminal justice system

Nursing and Midwifery Council.d: The NMC is the professional regulatory body for nurses, midwives, and nursing associates in the UK. It sets standards for education and practice, maintains a register of professionals, and investigates fitness-to-practise concerns.
Nursing and Midwifery Council.b: Patients through safe nursing and midwifery care, registered nurses and midwives, and student nurses

Museums and Galleries (Subgroup).d: Aggregate spending on national museums and galleries funded by DCMS and other departments, including operational grants, capital maintenance, and free admission policies for permanent collections.
Museums and Galleries (Subgroup).b: Museum and gallery visitors, curators, cultural heritage, and communities accessing free national collections

Office of Qualifications and Examination.d: Ofqual regulates qualifications, examinations, and assessments in England. It oversees GCSEs, A-levels, vocational qualifications, and ensures standards are maintained. It accredits awarding organisations and monitors exam delivery.
Office of Qualifications and Examination.b: Students taking regulated qualifications, schools, colleges, exam boards, and employers relying on qualification standards

Advanced Research and Invention Agency.d: ARIA is a new UK research funding agency modelled on DARPA/ARPA, empowering programme directors to fund high-risk, high-reward scientific research. It operates with minimal bureaucracy and has freedom to pursue transformative research agendas.
Advanced Research and Invention Agency.b: Frontier researchers, innovative scientists, the UK science base, and long-term technological advancement

Great British Nuclear Limited.d: GBN was established to drive delivery of new nuclear power in the UK, running competitions for Small Modular Reactor (SMR) technology selection, coordinating site readiness, and accelerating the nuclear new-build programme.
Great British Nuclear Limited.b: The nuclear energy industry, energy consumers, communities near nuclear sites, and net zero targets

United Kingdom Government Investments Li.d: UKGI is the government’s centre of excellence for corporate finance and governance, managing the government’s shareholder interests in businesses like NatWest Group, Channel 4, the Met Office, and other government-owned companies.
United Kingdom Government Investments Li.b: Taxpayers through effective management of government assets, and government-owned companies through governance oversight

National Lottery Commission.d: Now merged into the Gambling Commission (2013), it was responsible for granting and overseeing the licence to run the National Lottery, ensuring it was run with propriety, and protecting players’ interests.
National Lottery Commission.b: National Lottery players, good causes funded by lottery proceeds, and the lottery operator

Social Work England.d: The specialist regulator for social workers in England, maintaining the register of social workers, setting professional standards, and taking action when social workers fall below required standards.
Social Work England.b: People receiving social work services, social workers, student social workers, and employers of social workers

Commission for Local Administration CLG.d: The Local Government and Social Care Ombudsman investigates complaints about councils, adult social care providers, and some other local bodies. It provides free, independent dispute resolution for local government complaints.
Commission for Local Administration CLG.b: Residents with complaints about local councils, council tax payers, social care users, and local government

The Pensions Ombudsman.d: An independent body that investigates and determines complaints and disputes about pension schemes. It resolves pension disputes for free, with decisions legally binding on pension schemes.
The Pensions Ombudsman.b: Pension scheme members with complaints, pension scheme trustees, and employers running pension schemes

6.4 Street lighting.d: Government expenditure on street lighting infrastructure, including installation, maintenance, energy costs, and LED upgrade programmes for public highway lighting managed by local authorities.
6.4 Street lighting.b: Pedestrians, road users, local communities, and public safety through illuminated streets

Health Research Authority.d: The HRA protects and promotes the interests of patients and the public in health and social care research. It reviews research ethics applications, ensures research governance, and operates Research Ethics Committees.
Health Research Authority.b: Research participants, patients, clinical researchers, the NHS, and the research ethics community

Royal Air Force Museum.d: The RAF Museum operates sites at Hendon (London) and Cosford (Shropshire), preserving the history of the Royal Air Force. It holds major collections of aircraft, aviation artefacts, and archives documenting military aviation history.
Royal Air Force Museum.b: Museum visitors, aviation enthusiasts, military historians, school groups, and RAF veterans

Armed Forces Covenant Fund Trustee Ltd.d: Manages the Armed Forces Covenant Fund, distributing grants to support the armed forces community. Funds projects addressing veteran welfare, transition to civilian life, and community integration for service personnel and families.
Armed Forces Covenant Fund Trustee Ltd.b: Armed forces veterans, serving personnel, military families, and armed forces charities

Office for Environmental Protection.d: The OEP was established post-Brexit to replace EU oversight of environmental law compliance. It scrutinises environmental legislation implementation, investigates government failures on environmental law, and advises on environmental improvement.
Office for Environmental Protection.b: The environment, the public through environmental accountability, and environmental law compliance

Information Commission.d: The Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) is the UK’s independent data protection authority. It upholds information rights under GDPR/UK DPA 2018, the Freedom of Information Act, and related legislation. It investigates data breaches and issues fines.
Information Commission.b: All UK data subjects, organisations processing personal data, FOI requesters, and journalists

United Kingdom Anti Doping.d: UKAD is the national organisation responsible for protecting clean sport in the UK. It conducts drug testing, educates athletes, investigates doping violations, and manages results management for anti-doping rule violations.
United Kingdom Anti Doping.b: Clean athletes, sports integrity, sports governing bodies, and fair competition

Atomic Weapons Establishment plc.d: AWE (now AWE plc managed under a government-owned model since 2021) designs, manufactures, and maintains the UK’s nuclear warheads at sites in Aldermaston and Burghfield, Berkshire. It is central to the UK’s Continuous At-Sea Deterrent (Trident).
Atomic Weapons Establishment plc.b: National defence and security, the nuclear deterrent programme, defence workers, and allied nations under the nuclear umbrella

LocatED PLC.d: A government-owned property company that finds, acquires, and develops sites for free schools in England. It acts as the property arm of the Department for Education’s free schools programme.
LocatED PLC.b: Free school promoters, communities gaining new schools, parents, and pupils attending new free schools

Westminster Foundation for Democracy.d: WFD is a UK public body that supports democracy, good governance, and human rights worldwide. It works with parliaments, political parties, and civil society in developing countries to strengthen democratic institutions.
Westminster Foundation for Democracy.b: Developing country parliaments, political parties abroad, civil society organisations, and democratic governance globally

Trade Remedies Authority.d: The TRA investigates trade remedy cases for the UK post-Brexit, conducting anti-dumping, anti-subsidy, and safeguard investigations. It replaced the EU’s role in protecting UK industries from unfair international trade practices.
Trade Remedies Authority.b: UK domestic industries facing unfair imports, UK manufacturers, workers in affected sectors, and fair trade

Civil Nuclear Police Authority and Const.d: The CNC provides armed policing at civil nuclear sites across the UK, protecting nuclear materials and facilities. The CNPA oversees the constabulary’s governance. Officers are armed and trained in counter-terrorism.
Civil Nuclear Police Authority and Const.b: Civil nuclear site security, the public through nuclear safety, nuclear industry workers, and national security

House of Commons: Members.d: Spending on Members of Parliament including salaries, staffing allowances, office costs, travel expenses, and accommodation costs administered by IPSA since 2010.
House of Commons: Members.b: Members of Parliament, their staff, constituents receiving representation, and the democratic process

National Army Museum.d: The National Army Museum in Chelsea, London, tells the story of the British Army and its soldiers from the English Civil War to the present day. It holds collections of uniforms, weapons, art, and personal accounts.
National Army Museum.b: Museum visitors, military enthusiasts, school groups, army veterans, and military historians

Committee on Climate Change.d: The Climate Change Committee (CCC) is an independent statutory body advising the UK government on emissions targets and progress toward net zero. It produces annual progress reports to Parliament and recommends carbon budgets.
Committee on Climate Change.b: Government policy makers, the environment, future generations, and the public through climate accountability

Consumer Council for Water.d: CCWater represents water and sewerage customers in England and Wales. It investigates complaints, provides consumer advice, and challenges water companies and Ofwat on behalf of customers regarding service quality, pricing, and investment.
Consumer Council for Water.b: Water and sewerage customers, particularly vulnerable consumers and those experiencing service failures

Royal Armouries.d: Royal Armouries manages the national collection of arms and armour across three sites: the Tower of London, Royal Armouries Museum in Leeds, and Fort Nelson in Hampshire. It holds one of the oldest museum collections in the world.
Royal Armouries.b: Museum visitors, military historians, researchers, school groups, and heritage tourism

Northern Ireland Housing Executive.d: The NIHE is Northern Ireland’s strategic housing authority and the region’s largest landlord, managing approximately 85,000 homes. It administers Housing Benefit, assesses housing need, and delivers housing programmes across Northern Ireland.
Northern Ireland Housing Executive.b: Social housing tenants in Northern Ireland, those on housing waiting lists, and homeless individuals

HM Procurator General and Treasury Solic.d: Now known as the Government Legal Department (GLD), it is the government’s principal legal adviser providing litigation and advisory services to most government departments and bodies across England and Wales.
HM Procurator General and Treasury Solic.b: Government departments, ministers, civil servants, and the justice system through government legal representation

Single Source Regulations Office.d: The SSRO regulates single source defence contracts (non-competitive MoD procurement). It sets profit rates, oversees pricing, and ensures value for money when defence equipment is procured from a sole supplier.
Single Source Regulations Office.b: Taxpayers through defence procurement value, the MoD, and defence contractors through transparent pricing

National Forest Company.d: The NFC oversees the creation of the National Forest, a 200-square-mile area of new woodland across parts of Derbyshire, Leicestershire, and Staffordshire. It has transformed former coalfield landscapes through planting over 9 million trees.
National Forest Company.b: Local communities in the National Forest area, visitors, wildlife, timber industry, and the environment

Horserace Betting Levy Board.d: The HBLB collects a statutory levy from bookmakers on horserace betting to fund prize money, racecourse improvements, veterinary science, and the breeding industry. It supports the financial sustainability of British horseracing.
Horserace Betting Levy Board.b: The horseracing industry, racecourses, jockeys, horse breeders, stable staff, and veterinary researchers

3.1 of which: other police services.d: Police spending beyond the main police grant, including specialist police services, police support functions, police technology, and police services not classified under the primary police forces category.
3.1 of which: other police services.b: All citizens through policing, police forces, specialist law enforcement units, and communities

Valuation Tribunal Service CLG Main.d: The VTS provides administrative support to the Valuation Tribunal for England, which hears appeals on council tax banding and business rates valuations. It ensures independent adjudication of property valuation disputes.
Valuation Tribunal Service CLG Main.b: Council tax payers disputing their band, business ratepayers, and local authorities

RC UK Shared Services Centre Limited.d: A shared services company providing back-office support functions (HR, finance, IT, procurement) to UK Research Councils (now part of UKRI), delivering economies of scale across research funding bodies.
RC UK Shared Services Centre Limited.b: UK Research and Innovation, research councils, and taxpayers through efficient shared services

Oil & Gas Authority.d: Now the North Sea Transition Authority (NSTA, renamed 2022), it regulates and influences the UK oil, gas, and carbon storage industries. It issues licences, stewards the UK Continental Shelf, and supports energy transition.
Oil & Gas Authority.b: Oil and gas companies, energy consumers, coastal communities, and the energy transition sector

Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Of.d: Pension payments and obligations for former overseas civil servants who served in British colonies and dependent territories before independence. A legacy commitment paying pensions to retired colonial service officials.
Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Of.b: Retired former colonial and overseas civil servants, their widows, and dependants

Construction Industry Training Board.d: CITB is a statutory training body for the construction industry. It collects a levy from construction employers and invests in skills training, apprenticeships, and workforce development for the construction sector.
Construction Industry Training Board.b: Construction workers, apprentices, construction companies, and the built environment through skilled labour

Judicial Appointments Commission.d: The JAC selects candidates for judicial office in England and Wales on merit through fair and open competition. It is independent of government and makes recommendations to the Lord Chancellor for appointment.
Judicial Appointments Commission.b: Judicial candidates, the justice system through merit-based appointments, and public confidence in the judiciary

Criminal Cases Review Commission.d: The CCRC investigates suspected miscarriages of justice in England, Wales, and Northern Ireland. It reviews possible wrongful convictions and sentences and can refer cases back to the appeal courts when there is a real possibility of the conviction being overturned.
Criminal Cases Review Commission.b: People who may have been wrongly convicted, the justice system, and public confidence in criminal justice

Office for Budget Responsibility.d: The OBR provides independent analysis of the UK’s public finances. It produces economic and fiscal forecasts, assesses the government’s performance against fiscal targets, and analyses the long-term sustainability of public finances.
Office for Budget Responsibility.b: Parliament, government, financial markets, economists, journalists, and the public through fiscal transparency

Wales Audit Office.d: Now Audit Wales, it audits public bodies in Wales including the Welsh Government, local authorities, NHS bodies, and other Welsh public organisations. It provides independent assurance on the use of public money in Wales.
Wales Audit Office.b: Welsh taxpayers, the Senedd, Welsh public bodies, and the public through accountability of Welsh public spending

National Museum of the Royal Navy.d: NMRN manages a family of naval museums including the historic dockyards at Portsmouth and the Fleet Air Arm Museum at Yeovilton. It preserves naval heritage including HMS Victory, HMS Warrior, and submarine exhibits.
National Museum of the Royal Navy.b: Museum visitors, naval veterans, military historians, school groups, and maritime heritage

National Heritage Memorial Fund.d: NHMF provides emergency funding to save the most important heritage items and sites at risk for the nation. It acts as a fund of last resort when heritage is threatened with loss, dispersal, or destruction.
National Heritage Memorial Fund.b: The nation’s heritage, museums acquiring important objects, historic estates at risk, and cultural heritage

British Business Bank plc BEIS.d: The BEIS-linked arm of the British Business Bank reflecting its funding relationship with the former Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy. Same entity as BBB plc, facilitating access to finance for SMEs.
British Business Bank plc BEIS.b: Small and medium-sized enterprises, start-ups, and growth businesses seeking finance

United Kingdom Supreme Court.d: The UKSC is the final court of appeal for civil cases across the UK and criminal cases from England, Wales, and Northern Ireland. It hears cases of the greatest public and constitutional importance, replacing the Appellate Committee of the House of Lords.
United Kingdom Supreme Court.b: Parties in legal appeals, the legal profession, constitutional law, and the justice system

Northern Lighthouse Board.d: The NLB is the general lighthouse authority for Scotland and the Isle of Man, operating and maintaining over 200 lighthouses, buoys, and other navigational aids. It ensures the safety of navigation in Scottish and Manx waters.
Northern Lighthouse Board.b: Mariners, shipping companies, fishermen, and maritime safety in Scottish and Manx waters

National Portrait Gallery.d: The NPG in London houses a collection of portraits of historically important and famous British people. Recently underwent a major renovation (reopened 2023). It holds paintings, photographs, sculptures, and digital portraits.
National Portrait Gallery.b: Art lovers, portrait enthusiasts, tourists, researchers, school groups, and the general public

Marshall Aid Commemoration Commission.d: The MACC administers Marshall Scholarships, enabling outstanding American graduates to study at UK universities. Named after the Marshall Plan, the scholarships strengthen the UK-US academic and diplomatic relationship.
Marshall Aid Commemoration Commission.b: American graduate students, UK universities, and UK-US academic and diplomatic relations

Competition Service.d: The Competition Service provides administrative support to the Competition Appeal Tribunal (CAT), which hears appeals against CMA decisions, competition damages claims, and regulatory appeals.
Competition Service.b: Parties in competition law appeals, the legal profession, and fair competition through independent adjudication

Office of the Immigration Services Commi.d: The OISC regulates immigration advisers in the UK who are not supervised by a professional body. It ensures immigration advice and services are provided competently and honestly, and investigates complaints and illegal immigration advice.
Office of the Immigration Services Commi.b: Immigration applicants, asylum seekers, migrants seeking advice, and legitimate immigration advisers

Transport Focus.d: An independent watchdog representing transport users in Britain. It researches passenger satisfaction, investigates complaints escalated from operators, and advocates for improvement across rail, road, bus, and ferry services.
Transport Focus.b: Rail passengers, bus users, road users, and all public transport consumers

Public Service Ombudsman Wales.d: The PSOW investigates complaints about public bodies in Wales including local councils, the NHS in Wales, housing associations, and the Welsh Government. It provides an independent complaint resolution mechanism.
Public Service Ombudsman Wales.b: Welsh residents with complaints about public services, council tax payers, NHS patients in Wales

Horniman Museum.d: The Horniman Museum and Gardens in Forest Hill, south London, holds collections of anthropology, natural history, and musical instruments. It also features an aquarium, butterfly house, and 16 acres of gardens.
Horniman Museum.b: Museum visitors, local communities in south London, school groups, researchers, and families

Gangmasters Licensing Authority.d: Now the Gangmasters and Labour Abuse Authority (GLAA), it regulates labour providers (gangmasters) in agriculture, shellfish gathering, and food processing. It also investigates labour exploitation and modern slavery across all sectors.
Gangmasters Licensing Authority.b: Vulnerable workers, migrant workers, agricultural labourers, and legitimate labour providers

Regulator of Social Housing.d: RSH regulates registered providers of social housing in England, including housing associations. It sets economic standards for governance and financial viability and consumer standards for tenant services and housing quality.
Regulator of Social Housing.b: Social housing tenants, housing associations, the social housing sector, and local communities

Churches Conservation Trust.d: The CCT cares for over 350 Church of England churches no longer needed for regular worship. It conserves these historic buildings, makes them accessible to the public, and finds new uses for them as community spaces, concert venues, and tourism destinations.
Churches Conservation Trust.b: Heritage visitors, local communities, architectural heritage, and the Church of England

Midlands Engine Investments Limited.d: A government-backed investment fund supporting business growth in the Midlands region through equity and debt investments. Part of the Midlands Engine initiative to boost economic growth and productivity in the East and West Midlands.
Midlands Engine Investments Limited.b: Midlands businesses, entrepreneurs, regional economic growth, and communities in the Midlands

Health Services Safety Investigations Bo.d: HSSIB (now established as a statutory body) conducts independent safety investigations into NHS patient safety incidents in England. It identifies systemic risks and makes recommendations to improve healthcare safety without apportioning blame.
Health Services Safety Investigations Bo.b: NHS patients through safer care, healthcare workers, and the health system through learning from incidents

Independent Monitoring Authority for the.d: The IMA monitors the implementation and application of the citizens’ rights parts of the EU Withdrawal Agreement and EEA EFTA Separation Agreement, ensuring EU/EEA/Swiss citizens’ rights in the UK are upheld post-Brexit.
Independent Monitoring Authority for the.b: EU, EEA, and Swiss citizens living in the UK, and UK citizens in the EU whose rights are reciprocally protected

Covent Garden Market Authority.d: CGMA operates New Covent Garden Market in Nine Elms, London, the UK’s largest wholesale fruit, vegetable, and flower market. It manages the market site and its redevelopment, and provides facilities for market traders.
Covent Garden Market Authority.b: Wholesale traders, florists, restaurants, fruit and vegetable retailers, and London’s food supply chain

BIS (Postal Services Act 2011) Company L.d: A government-owned company established under the Postal Services Act 2011 to manage the government’s residual interests and obligations relating to the privatisation of Royal Mail and the Post Office pension arrangements.
BIS (Postal Services Act 2011) Company L.b: Taxpayers through management of Royal Mail privatisation legacy, and Post Office pension scheme members

Children’s Commissioner.d: The Children’s Commissioner for England promotes and protects the rights of children, particularly the most vulnerable. The office conducts research, raises awareness of children’s issues, and represents children’s views to government and public bodies.
Children’s Commissioner.b: All children in England, especially vulnerable and disadvantaged children, and child welfare policy

6.5 R&D housing and community amenities.d: Research and development spending on housing and community amenities, including building standards research, construction technology, housing policy research, and community development studies.
6.5 R&D housing and community amenities.b: The housing sector, construction industry, housing researchers, and communities through improved housing knowledge

Independent Commission for Aid Impact.d: ICAI independently scrutinises UK Official Development Assistance spending on behalf of Parliament. It evaluates aid effectiveness, value for money, and impact, publishing reviews and recommendations.
Independent Commission for Aid Impact.b: Aid recipients in developing countries, taxpayers, Parliament, and the international development sector through accountability

Security Industry Authority.d: The SIA regulates the private security industry in the UK, licensing door supervisors, security guards, CCTV operators, and other private security workers. It sets and enforces standards for the security industry.
Security Industry Authority.b: The public through regulated security services, licensed security workers, and businesses employing security staff

4.4 Mining, manufacturing and constructi.d: Government spending on mining, manufacturing, and construction sectors including regulatory oversight, industrial support programmes, building regulations, and construction industry policy.
4.4 Mining, manufacturing and constructi.b: Mining companies, manufacturers, construction firms, workers in these industries, and consumers of manufactured goods

Financial Reporting Council Limited.d: The FRC (being replaced by ARGA - Audit, Reporting and Governance Authority) regulates auditors, accountants, and actuaries. It sets accounting and auditing standards, oversees corporate governance, and promotes transparency in business reporting.
Financial Reporting Council Limited.b: Investors, company shareholders, auditors, accountants, and financial markets through reliable reporting

Leasehold Advisory Service.d: LEASE provides free legal advice to leaseholders, freeholders, and property managers on residential leasehold and commonhold law. It helps people understand their rights and obligations under leasehold legislation.
Leasehold Advisory Service.b: Residential leaseholders, freeholders, property managers, and those considering leasehold property transactions

NI Human Rights Commission.d: The NIHRC promotes awareness and protection of human rights in Northern Ireland. Established under the Belfast/Good Friday Agreement, it advises government, reviews legislation, and can take human rights cases to court.
NI Human Rights Commission.b: All people in Northern Ireland, human rights defenders, victims of rights violations, and the peace process

The Housing Ombudsman.d: The Housing Ombudsman resolves disputes between tenants of social landlords (housing associations and local authority arms-length management organisations) and their landlords in England.
The Housing Ombudsman.b: Social housing tenants with complaints, housing associations, and the social housing sector

The Office for Place.d: A small body within DLUHC promoting beauty and good design in the built environment. It provides guidance on placemaking, design codes, and community engagement in planning and development processes.
The Office for Place.b: Local communities, planners, developers, and residents through better-designed places

Electricity Settlements Company.d: A government-owned company managing financial settlement of Contracts for Difference (CfD) payments for low-carbon electricity generation. It collects the CfD levy from suppliers and makes payments to renewable generators.
Electricity Settlements Company.b: Renewable energy generators, electricity consumers (through clean energy), and the net zero transition

Football Licensing Authority.d: Now the Sports Grounds Safety Authority (SGSA, renamed 2011), it was established after the Hillsborough disaster to oversee safety at football grounds and other sports venues in England and Wales through licensing and inspection.
Football Licensing Authority.b: Football spectators, sports ground attendees, ground safety officers, and sports clubs

Health and Care Professions Council.d: The HCPC regulates 15 health and care professions in the UK including physiotherapists, paramedics, occupational therapists, and social workers (social workers moved to SWE in 2019). It maintains registers and investigates fitness-to-practise concerns.
Health and Care Professions Council.b: Patients, service users, registered health professionals, and the public through regulated healthcare practitioners

Human Tissue Authority.d: The HTA regulates the removal, storage, use, and disposal of human bodies, organs, and tissue for purposes including transplantation, research, education, and public display. It licenses establishments and ensures ethical treatment of human tissue.
Human Tissue Authority.b: Organ transplant patients, research participants, pathology services, anatomical science, and bereaved families

Wallace Collection.d: A national museum in Hertford House, London, holding an outstanding collection of fine and decorative arts including Old Master paintings, arms and armour, porcelain, and 18th-century French furniture. Admission is free.
Wallace Collection.b: Art enthusiasts, museum visitors, researchers, tourists, and the public through free access to a world-class collection

Local Government Boundary Commission for.d: LGBCE conducts electoral reviews of local authority boundaries in England, drawing ward boundaries, determining council sizes, and conducting reviews of local government structures to ensure effective representation.
Local Government Boundary Commission for.b: Voters through fair representation, local authorities, councillors, and the democratic process

Crown Estate Office.d: The Crown Estate manages a GBP15.6 billion property portfolio owned by the monarch in right of the Crown. It manages the seabed (including offshore wind leases), Regent Street, and rural and urban estates. Profits go to HM Treasury.
Crown Estate Office.b: The Treasury (through revenue), offshore wind developers, commercial tenants, and the public through Crown Estate profits

Engineering Construction Industry Traini.d: ECITB is the statutory skills body for the engineering construction industry. It collects a levy from employers and invests in training, apprenticeships, and skills development for power generation, oil and gas, and process industries.
Engineering Construction Industry Traini.b: Engineering construction workers, apprentices, employers in energy and process industries, and the engineering skills pipeline

Human Fertilisation and Embryology Autho.d: The HFEA regulates fertility clinics and research involving human embryos in the UK. It licenses IVF clinics, sets standards for treatment, maintains a register of treatments and donors, and oversees embryo research ethics.
Human Fertilisation and Embryology Autho.b: Patients undergoing fertility treatment, donor-conceived people, embryo researchers, and IVF clinics

Export Credits Guarantee Department.d: Now trading as UK Export Finance (UKEF), it is the UK’s export credit agency. It provides insurance, guarantees, and loans to support UK exports, helping British companies win overseas contracts and manage export risks.
Export Credits Guarantee Department.b: UK exporters, overseas buyers of British goods, SMEs entering export markets, and the UK economy

6.1 of which: local authority housing (G.d: Smaller-value local authority housing expenditure entries, likely representing minor maintenance, estate management costs, or adjustments in local authority housing accounts.
6.1 of which: local authority housing (G.b: Council housing tenants and local authority housing departments

Geffrye Museum.d: Now the Museum of the Home (renamed 2019), located in Hackney, east London. It explores the home and domestic life through 400 years, with period room displays, gardens, and exhibitions about how people have lived.
Geffrye Museum.b: Museum visitors, local Hackney community, design enthusiasts, social historians, and families

Water Services Regulation Authority.d: Ofwat regulates the water and sewerage sectors in England and Wales. It sets price controls for water companies, ensures service quality, promotes competition, and protects consumers’ interests in the water industry.
Water Services Regulation Authority.b: Water consumers, households, businesses, the environment (through water company environmental obligations), and the water industry

NI Parades Commission.d: The Parades Commission for Northern Ireland regulates public processions and related protests in Northern Ireland. It issues determinations on contentious parades, imposing conditions to minimise community tensions.
NI Parades Commission.b: Northern Irish communities, parade organisers (Orange Order, etc.), residents on parade routes, and community relations

Agriculture & Horticulture Development B.d: AHDB is a statutory levy board funded by farmers and growers in England. It invests in research, market development, knowledge exchange, and export promotion across beef, lamb, pork, cereals, oilseeds, dairy, and horticulture sectors.
Agriculture & Horticulture Development B.b: Farmers, growers, the food supply chain, agricultural researchers, and UK food exports

Office of Rail and Road.d: ORR regulates the rail industry’s health and safety, Network Rail’s performance and efficiency, and access to the railway network. Post-2015 it also monitors Highways England’s (now National Highways) management of strategic roads.
Office of Rail and Road.b: Rail passengers, freight operators, railway workers, road users, and the rail and roads industries

Government Actuary’s Department.d: GAD provides actuarial analysis, advice, and assurance to the public sector, including pension scheme valuations, insurance analysis, and demographic modelling for government departments and public bodies.
Government Actuary’s Department.b: Government departments, public sector pension schemes, social security policy, and taxpayers through informed actuarial decisions

Sir John Soane’s Museum.d: A house museum at Lincoln’s Inn Fields, London, preserved as architect Sir John Soane left it in 1837. It contains his collection of antiquities, architectural models, paintings (including Hogarth’s A Rake’s Progress), and drawings. Free admission.
Sir John Soane’s Museum.b: Museum visitors, architecture students, art historians, tourists, and the public

Cornwall and Isles of Scilly Investments.d: A government-backed investment fund supporting business growth in Cornwall and the Isles of Scilly, providing equity and loan finance to SMEs in one of the UK’s less economically developed regions.
Cornwall and Isles of Scilly Investments.b: Cornwall and Isles of Scilly businesses, entrepreneurs, local economy, and communities in the region

Train Fleet.d: Government spending on rolling stock for the rail network, particularly relevant during the COVID-19 period when the government took on greater responsibility for train leasing and procurement to maintain rail services.
Train Fleet.b: Rail passengers, train operating companies, rolling stock leasing companies, and rail manufacturing

Great Britain China Centre.d: The GBCC promotes understanding between Britain and China through cultural, educational, and governance exchanges. It runs programmes on rule of law, judicial reform, and governance cooperation.
Great Britain China Centre.b: UK-China diplomatic relations, legal and governance professionals, academic institutions, and bilateral cultural exchange

Independent Reporting Commission.d: The IRC was established by the UK and Irish governments and Northern Ireland parties to report on progress towards ending paramilitary activity in Northern Ireland, as part of the Fresh Start Agreement commitments.
Independent Reporting Commission.b: Northern Irish communities affected by paramilitarism, the peace process, and victims of paramilitary activity

3.5 R&D public order and safety.d: Research and development spending on public order and safety, including policing research, forensic science development, criminal justice research, and security technology innovation.
3.5 R&D public order and safety.b: Law enforcement agencies, forensic scientists, criminal justice researchers, and the public through improved safety technology

Office for Legal Complaints.d: The OLC oversees the Legal Ombudsman, which resolves complaints about lawyers (solicitors, barristers, and other legal professionals) in England and Wales. It provides a free, independent complaint resolution service.
Office for Legal Complaints.b: Consumers of legal services with complaints, solicitors, barristers, and the legal profession

Commissioners of Irish Lights.d: CIL is the general lighthouse authority for Ireland (Republic and Northern Ireland). Jointly funded by the UK and Irish governments, it maintains lighthouses, buoys, and navigational aids around the coast of Ireland.
Commissioners of Irish Lights.b: Mariners navigating Irish waters, shipping companies, fishermen, and maritime safety around Ireland

Sea Fish Industry Authority.d: Seafish is a non-departmental public body funded by a levy on the UK seafood industry. It supports responsible sourcing, promotes seafood consumption, conducts fisheries research, and provides industry training and guidance.
Sea Fish Industry Authority.b: Fishermen, seafood processors, fish retailers, consumers, and the UK fishing industry

Trinity House Lighthouse Service.d: Trinity House is the general lighthouse authority for England, Wales, the Channel Islands, and Gibraltar. It maintains lighthouses, lightships, buoys, and electronic navigation aids. It also manages a charitable fund for mariners.
Trinity House Lighthouse Service.b: Mariners, shipping companies, recreational sailors, and maritime safety in English and Welsh waters

9.7 R&D education.d: Research and development spending on education, including educational research, pedagogy studies, educational technology development, and evidence-based education policy research funded through the Education Endowment Foundation and research councils.
9.7 R&D education.b: Teachers, students, education researchers, schools, and education policy through evidence-based practice

Professional Standards Authority for Hea.d: The PSA oversees the nine health and care professional regulatory bodies in the UK (including GMC, NMC, HCPC). It audits their performance, can appeal unduly lenient fitness-to-practise decisions, and accredits voluntary registers.
Professional Standards Authority for Hea.b: Patients and service users through oversight of regulators, health professionals, and public confidence in healthcare

Legal Services Board.d: The LSB is the independent body responsible for overseeing the regulation of lawyers in England and Wales. It oversees the frontline regulators (SRA, BSB, etc.) and promotes the regulatory objectives set out in the Legal Services Act 2007.
Legal Services Board.b: Legal services consumers, solicitors, barristers, legal regulators, and the justice system

HM Treasury UK Sovereign SUKUK plc.d: A special purpose vehicle established by HM Treasury to issue the UK’s first sovereign Islamic bond (sukuk). The UK was the first Western country to issue sovereign sukuk, demonstrating London’s role as a centre for Islamic finance.
HM Treasury UK Sovereign SUKUK plc.b: Islamic finance investors, the UK Islamic finance sector, and the Treasury through diversified funding sources

IUK Investments.d: Part of UK Infrastructure Bank/Innovate UK, providing equity and debt investments in UK infrastructure projects. It supports clean energy, digital infrastructure, and transport projects aligned with net zero and levelling up goals.
IUK Investments.b: Infrastructure project developers, clean energy companies, digital connectivity projects, and communities benefiting from infrastructure investment

Royal Mint Advisory Committee on the des.d: The RMAC advises the Chancellor on the designs of UK coins, commemorative coins, and medals. It reviews and recommends designs for circulation and collector coins, ensuring artistic quality and historical accuracy.
Royal Mint Advisory Committee on the des.b: Coin collectors, numismatists, the Royal Mint, and the public through well-designed coinage

London and Continental Railways.d: LCR was the government-owned company responsible for the Channel Tunnel Rail Link (HS1) and the regeneration of King’s Cross and Stratford areas. It managed major property development and rail infrastructure before being wound down.
London and Continental Railways.b: HS1 rail passengers, Eurostar users, King’s Cross and Stratford communities, and property development beneficiaries

Net Zero Teesside.d: A major carbon capture, usage, and storage (CCUS) project on Teesside, aiming to create the UK’s first decarbonised industrial cluster. It captures CO2 from industrial processes and stores it beneath the North Sea.
Net Zero Teesside.b: Teesside industrial workers, the net zero transition, local communities, and heavy industry decarbonisation

British Technology Investments.d: A subsidiary of the British Business Bank focused on making equity investments in innovative, high-growth UK technology companies. Part of the government’s strategy to keep tech companies listed and growing in the UK.
British Technology Investments.b: UK technology companies, tech entrepreneurs, the innovation economy, and investors in British tech

8.6 Recreation, culture and religion n.e.d: Recreation, culture, and religion spending not elsewhere classified, including miscellaneous cultural programmes, recreation policy administration, and cultural initiatives that do not fit other specific sub-categories.
8.6 Recreation, culture and religion n.e.b: Cultural organisations, recreation providers, and the general public

10.1 Sickness and disability; of which:.d: Cash benefits for people unable to work due to sickness or disability, including Employment and Support Allowance, Personal Independence Payment, Disability Living Allowance, and Industrial Injuries Disablement Benefit.
